what_it_does: This file is a channel attachment metadata record that links an image asset to the design-source channel as an images attachment type, recording an id, target URI, and timestamp.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, with no body content
Fields include id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added timestamp
Target uses an image:// URI pointing to design-source/recent-pitches/p-13
Added timestamp is ISO 8601 with microsecond precision and timezone offset
No code symbols or executable content
standouts: Target URI uses a custom image:// scheme
Attachment id follows an att- prefix pattern
concerns: Target URI cannot be resolved or validated from this file alone
No title, description, or author for the attachment
No indication of attachment lifecycle or status
notes: A lightweight provenance stub that registers an image attachment in the design-source channel.
questions: What does the image at recent-pitches/p-13 depict?
Who added this attachment and why?
Is the image:// URI scheme resolvable by the consuming system?
What is the lifecycle or current status of this attachment?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is an images attachment for the design-source channel targeting recent-pitches/p-13, and the file content matches that purpose.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to create a timestamped, typed reference that records when an image asset was attached to a channel, supporting retrieval and provenance tracking for design pitches.
contribution: Provides a timestamped, typed link between a channel and an external image asset, which a fused system would keep as a provenance and attachment reference.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a YAML metadata attachment record that links the design-source channel to the image asset recent-pitches/p-13 with an id and timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for cataloging an image attachment in the design-source channel to support retrieval, organization, and audit.
